key_themes: Strong Skyrizi/Rinvoq growth driving revenue beats and offsetting Humira erosion
Recent 2026 EPS guidance cut from $744M IPR&D charge
Attractive valuation, high FCF/dividend yield (~3.3%), and moderate buy consensus
summary: AbbVie continues to post earnings beats (Q4 2025 EPS $2.71 beat estimates; revenue +10% YoY) with Skyrizi and Rinvoq powering growth, robust FCF supporting its dividend aristocrat status (~3.3% yield), and analysts holding a Moderate Buy rating with ~$249 average target (implying ~20% upside from ~$208).[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABBV/)[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/ABBV/) However, the early-April 2026 cut to FY EPS guidance ($13.96–$14.16 from prior $14.37–$14.57) due to a large Q1 IPR&D expense, combined with ongoing Humira declines, rising competition in immunology (e.g. J&J oral therapies), and a high earnings bar, tempers momentum.[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/abbvie-nyseabbv-issues-q1-2026-earnings-guidance-2026-04-09/)[[3]](https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/abbvie-reports-744-million-iprd-expense-for-q1-updates-2026-earnings-guidance-93CH-4597303)[[4]](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/ABBV/8-k-abb-vie-inc-reports-material-event-244b91cfdff9.html) Biggest risk is intensified competitive pressure on key growth drivers Skyrizi and Rinvoq alongside pipeline execution needs.[[5]](https://www.forbes.com/sites/greatspeculations/2026/03/19/buy-or-fear-abbvie-stock-at-210/)

recent_posts.highlight: High 3% yield, 54-year dividend streak, successful pivot to new drugs like Skyrizi
Buy rating; shares attractive on valuation and forward EPS growth despite 8% YTD decline and high 2026 bar
Strongly bearish daily trend; price stagnating
No-brainer stock to buy on the dip; highlighted in high-yield dividend lists
